title: 2nd Conjugation
purpose: Train present-tense 2nd-conjugation verbs, with explicit isolation of irregular я-forms.
uiSignature: Same pronoun strip as 1st conjugation, plus a compact irregular-я banner whenever the current verb is flagged irregular.
subdeckRules: Keep regular core verbs, exception verbs, and perfective-future reference verbs in separate subdecks.
Surface irregular я-form hints only in Preview and first two Guided attempts; remove them once the atom reaches Full Choice.
Perfective-future reference verbs must never appear in the default 2nd-conjugation Resume flow.

referenceTable.notes: Some verbs in the notes belong to the 2nd conjugation even though the infinitive does not end in -ить (e.g. помнить, слышать, видеть).

subdecks.title: Core 2nd-conjugation verbs
subdecks.whyThisExists: This is the main 2nd-conjugation module. It includes the verbs you clearly practiced in class, but still tags irregular я-forms so the UI can surface them.

verbs.translation: to boil / to cook by boiling

teachingRules: Choose2/Choose4/FullChoice options must stay inside the same verb paradigm.
Whenever the current atom has irregularYa=true, show the irregular-я helper banner in Preview and early Guided stages only.
Do not mix perfective-future verbs into this subdeck.

atoms.translation: to boil / to cook by boiling
to boil / to cook by boiling
to boil / to cook by boiling
to boil / to cook by boiling
to boil / to cook by boiling
to boil / to cook by boiling

subdecks.title: 2nd-conjugation exception verbs (not plain -ить pattern)
subdecks.whyThisExists: These verbs are pedagogically important because they are true 2nd-conjugation verbs, but their infinitives do not look like the core -ить pattern.
teachingRules: Keep this as a dedicated subdeck so the user can isolate exception verbs.
The app must still tag irregular я-forms inside this subdeck when relevant (especially видеть).

subdecks.title: Perfective future reference verbs (advanced / off by default)
subdecks.whyThisExists: The notes include several perfective verbs whose simple future forms look like 2nd-conjugation present forms. They should be available, but not mixed into present-tense conjugation practice by default.

verbs.translation: to turn on / switch on (perfective; future forms)
to turn off / switch off (perfective; future forms)

teachingRules: Label every card as simple future / perfective reference.
Do not include these atoms in default mixed review.

sentenceBank.ru: В четверг он говорит по телефону.
sentenceBank.en: On Thursday he speaks on the phone.
I speak a little Russian.

sentenceBank.ru: В среду я говорю с друзьями.
sentenceBank.en: On Wednesday I speak with friends.

sentenceBank.ru: В среду мы учим новые слова.
sentenceBank.en: On Wednesday we learn new words.
On Saturday I cook breakfast.
On Monday do you call your sister?
On Sunday do you ask for help?

sentenceBank.ru: По вечерам мы ходим в спортзал.
sentenceBank.en: In the evenings we go to the gym.
He usually wears a black jacket.
On Tuesday she cooks soup.
On Friday they salt the fish.
Sometimes she gives flowers to her mother.

sentenceBank.ru: В четверг мы строим планы на выходные.
sentenceBank.en: On Thursday we make plans for the weekend.

sentenceBank.ru: Мы всегда благодарим преподавателя за урок.
sentenceBank.en: We always thank the teacher for the lesson.

sentenceBank.ru: В среду она переводит текст с английского.
sentenceBank.en: On Wednesday she translates a text from English.

sentenceBank.ru: В понедельник я прихожу в офис рано.
sentenceBank.en: On Monday I arrive at the office early.

sentenceBank.ru: В пятницу они выходят из дома в восемь утра.
sentenceBank.en: On Friday they leave home at eight in the morning.
You are watching a film.

sentenceBank.ru: В понедельник ты помнишь о встрече?
sentenceBank.en: On Monday do you remember the meeting?
We remember this rule well.
We remember this.

sentenceBank.ru: Во вторник мы иногда слышим странный шум.
sentenceBank.en: On Tuesday we sometimes hear a strange noise.
She usually hears your voice.
sentenceBank.note: Canonicalized from a note fragment missing the subject.

sentenceBank.en: Do you hear the music?
We hear the dog.
They see the car.
I see you.
She loves sport and yoga.
I always love drinking coffee.
I love pizza.
sentenceBank.note: Reconstructed from the incomplete fill-in on the page.

sentenceBank.en: We love dancing.
sentenceBank.note: Reconstructed from the incomplete fill-in on the page.

sentenceBank.en: On Saturday I will review grammar.
He will explain the rule very clearly.
sentenceBank.note: Use without обычно. The original note mixed usually with a perfective future form.

sentenceBank.ru: В воскресенье вы встретите друзей в центре?
sentenceBank.en: Will you meet friends in the center on Sunday?

sentenceBank.ru: Во вторник ты включишь компьютер первым?
sentenceBank.en: On Tuesday will you turn on the computer first?

sentenceBank.ru: Иногда она выключит телефон на ночь.
sentenceBank.en: Sometimes she will turn off the phone for the night.
